description: Durable memory appends raw model output forever with no scoping or eviction, leaking data and growing without bound.
body: Failure mode: the agent writes every raw utterance into long-term memory with no validation, scope, or eviction, so memory grows unbounded, mixes data across sessions or users, and is later read back as trusted fact. Detection signals: append-on-every-step memory writes; no scope tag; no eviction or summarization policy; stored entries trusted blindly on retrieval. Do not persist unvalidated or cross-scope content. Safer fallback: write only validated, scoped facts, set an eviction or summarization policy, treat stored memory as untrusted input on read, and defer any PII handling to risk.privacy.
